Section.name: UNCLASSIFIED
Section.Content: Prepared by: Johan Pauwels Date: 13/03/2024 Cegeka reference: PRD.0.L.101 DV1.1 CONFIDENTIAL Service Description PRD.0.L.101 Cegeka Cloud – Housing Services Table of contents 1 Service Description 4 1.1 Service identification 4 1.2 Service summary 4 1.2.1 Infrastructure management 4 1.2.2 The Infrastructure 4 1.3 Service features 6 1.3.1 Standard Service features 6 1.3.1.1 Infrastructure knowledge 6 1.3.1.2 Technical setup 6 1.3.1.3 Technical management 6 1.3.1.4 Housekeeping 6 1.3.1.5 Monitoring 6 1.3.1.6 Security and Compliance 6 1.3.1.7 Customers responsibility 7 1.3.2 Optional Service features 7 1.3.3 Requests 8 1.3.4 Out of scope 8 1.4 Service characteristics 9 1.4.1 Obligatory 9 1.4.2 Cegeka owned Licenses and/or subscriptions 9 1.4.3 Customer owned Licenses and/or subscriptions 9 1.4.4 Conditions 9 1.4.5 Service delivery location 9 1.4.6 Service Reporting 10 1.4.7 Data classification 10 1.4.8 Use of Subcontractor/Sub-outsourcer 10 1.4.9 Miscellaneous 10 1.4.10 RACI Matrix 11 1.5 Adherence to Cross-Functional Services 12 2 Service Level Agreement 13 2.1 Service Window 13 2.2 Service Level 13 3 Service Billing 14 3.1 Standard Service Billing 14 3.2 Optional Service Billing 15 3.3 Change Request Billing 16 4 Document control 17 5 Document approval 18

Section.name: Service identification
Section.Content: Service Cegeka Cloud – Housing Services Unique ID PRD.0.L.101 Service Model Infrastructure management This service is a base service and is not dependant of other layers (and not dependant of other Service Descriptions).

Section.name: Infrastructure management
Section.Content: The Service provides the tasks and activities needed to manage the Cegeka Cloud Housing (The Infrastructure) from a technical perspective. Cegeka unburdens the customer with all technical aspects for this infrastructure. The Standard Service features are focussed to keep the Service up-and-running, to keep it secure and to optimize its technical parameters.

Section.name: The Infrastructure
Section.Content: The Cegeka Cloud Housing Service offers physical locations for the installation of hardware in the Cegeka datacentres. The physical location can be in the form of shared rack space, a closed dedicated rack or a cage. A cage is a closed fenced location in a Cegeka Datacentre which is only accessible by Cegeka Datacentre personnel and identified Customer contacts under supervision of Cegeka Datacentre personnel. The physical location can be used to house Customer dedicated equipment (servers, network equipment, storage, etc.) and can provide redundant power, redundant network and redundant SAN connectivity. Customer dedicated equipment: The Infrastructure includes the following components: Cegeka Datacentres are geographically spread over 2 locations: one in Belgium (Hasselt) and one in the Netherlands (Geleen). Cegeka Datacentres are Tier 3 certified, guaranteeing high availability, redundancy, operational continuity and safety and security. Cegeka provides also additional connectivity POP (Point of Presence) locations in Belgium (Zaventem) and the Netherlands (Oude Meer), where Customer network access endpoint equipment can be placed. The Cegeka Datacentres and POPs are interconnected by Cegeka private and redundant data connection lines. Bandwidth is continuous monitored to assure overcapacity for traffic. Please note that the locations and interconnection of the Cegeka Datacentres and POPs may change over time. Such changes are always communicated upfront to relevant Customers and do not impact the nature of this Service.

Section.name: Standard Service features
Section.Content: Cegeka ensures skilled and certified engineers with experience on all housing components in order to perform the Service. Cegeka works together with contracted vendors to provide continuity on these components. Cegeka continue evaluates and adapts the housing environment to ensure optimal technical setup (racks, cabling, physical security measurements, power supply, etc.). Cegeka advises the Customer on housing needs and proposes optimal solutions for each case. Cegeka performs and maintains the technical installation of the Infrastructure ensuring its correct working in accordance with Tier 3 Datacentre standards. Technical setup is documented on operational level but is not available to the Customer. It can be subject to formal Customer auditing upon prior announcement. Documents can be shown but are not distributed. Cegeka is responsible for the technical operation of the Infrastructure and to assure it’s correct running during the agreed availability windows. Recurrent maintenance activities (housekeeping) are needed to ensure the correct working of the Infrastructure. Automation is applied where possible. Proactive management Cegeka performs housekeeping activities on all aspects of the housing service. Resolution actions are triggered when needed and followed to assure the SLA. Cegeka monitors the correct working of the housing infrastructure on a 24/7 basis (power, climate, access, etc.) Alerts are treated and appropriate resolution actions are triggered and followed to assure the SLA. Physical security The housing locations and the Cegeka Datacentre and POP locations are permanent physically secured and monitored. Access is strictly arranged by the Cegeka Access Control procedure: Management of technical users The management of technical users (for engineers) is performed following the Cegeka internal security policies and the contractual Customer security policies. Detection of security incidents The security management is performed following the Cegeka internal security policies and the contractual Customer security policies. Security incidents are created in case a security breach is detected and is immediately communicated to the Customer and Cegeka stakeholders. SIEM SIEM is part of the Cegeka internal service and not deliverable to the Customer. Underlaying Platform hardening Not applicable for this Service. The Customer remains responsible for the equipment placed in the housing locations. This may be by the Customer itself, a Third Party or by an adjacent Cegeka Service.

Section.name: Optional Service features
Section.Content: This section describes the features that can be optionally added to the standard Service features and that will be priced (when applicable) on top of the standard Service features. The following optional features are possible with the Service:

Section.name: Requests
Section.Content: Both the Customer and Cegeka can issue a Request for Change (RFC) or a Request for Information (RFI). There are multiple types of requests: The handling of Requests is subject to the regular change management process (see Cross-functionals). request type Description Lead time Service Requests Access to a Cegeka Datacentre or POP for hardware installation or service purposes (Engineers) 24 hours Physical handling (power on/off, status/led check, etc) 1 hour Normal Changes Any other request. Examples: • Advise on housing • Hands-on assistance for hardware handling or connectivity handling (power, network, SAN, etc.) • Shipping, device handling, packaging, etc. Indicated case-by-case during the analysis of the request.

Section.name: Out of scope
Section.Content: Everything that is not explicitly mentioned in this service description is by default out of scope of this Service.

Section.name: Obligatory
Section.Content: The following services are mandatory for the delivery of this service:

Section.name: Cegeka owned Licenses and/or subscriptions
Section.Content: These licenses and/or subscriptions and their cost are included in this Service. It is Cegeka’s responsibility to maintain license and/or subscription compliance. Licenses and/or subscriptions used for Cross-Functional Services are included in the Service and are not listed below. License included in service applies to (none specific)

Section.name: Customer owned Licenses and/or subscriptions
Section.Content: These licenses and/or subscriptions and their cost are not included in this Service. It is the customers responsibility to maintain license and/or subscription compliance. License description/remarks (none specific)

Section.name: Service delivery location
Section.Content: This Service is delivered from within the physical Cegeka Datacentre and POP locations.

Section.name: Service Reporting
Section.Content: Monthly reporting will be provided to inform on the availability and meeting the SLA’s and to provide insights on the capacity and expected extensions required. Specific reports are provided:

Section.name: Data classification
Section.Content: Cegeka requires no access to confidential and/or personal data to perform this Service. Cegeka Datacentre personnel may see fragments of data in case it is shared by the Customer (data fragments in the sense of information on screens, etc.)

Section.name: Use of Subcontractor/Sub-outsourcer
Section.Content: This service is delivered with: Subcontractor (Cegeka manages the Service with own engineers which are supported by an external party for escalations or technology specific questions or issues): (none) Sub-outsourcer (Cegeka delegates the Service to an external party): Penta Datacenter Geleen (DC Geleen)

Section.name: Miscellaneous
Section.Content: n/a

Section.name: RACI Matrix
Section.Content: Activity Cegeka Customer Architecture & Design General architecture and design of all housing components A,R Plan & Deliver Build in/out of physical equipment, changes to connectivity of physical equipment A,R C Housekeeping (manage & assure) Environment and facilities support Cegeka Datacentres, including remote monitoring environmental systems, hands & eyes functionality, planning, implementation of Changes A,R I (when Customer is at risk) Security Physical access control A,R I RACI role definition:

Section.name: Adherence to Cross-Functional Services
Section.Content: The Service described in this Service Description adheres to the Cross-functional Services as described in the contract where this Service is residing unless documented in the table below: Cross-Functional Service Deviation from adherence to cross-functional services n/a The Cross -Functional services rely on the Cegeka standard framework for processes and tooling.

Section.name: Service Window
Section.Content: The Service Window is the time frame during which the agreed support services from Cegeka are available. Service windows are defined in the related Service Level Management document to this contract.

Section.name: Service Level
Section.Content: Service Levels of the cross-functional services are defined in the related Service Level Management document to this contract. The actual mapping of the operational service instances towards these KPI’s are documented on the Service Delivery site under ‘Operational documentation’. Availability The following Availability KPI’s are provided for the Service: KPI ID KPI name Availability Service Window Metric KPI.0.L.101.Gold DC availability Gold 99,98 % The availability measurement is monthly and calculated as:

Section.name: Standard Service Billing
Section.Content: The service is billed on a monthly basis. The actual mapping of the operational service instances towards these billing elements are documented on the Service Delivery site under ‘Operational documentation’. ID Component Billing element BILLING UNIT SKU.0.L.101.L01 Datacentre rack space Collocation Tier 3 - DC2011 - Hasselt (BE) 1U Rack and power SKU.0.L.101.L02 Collocation Tier 3 - DC1992 - Hasselt (BE) 1U Rack and power SKU.0.L.101.L11 Colocation Tier 3 - DC Geleen 1 - Geleen (NL) 1U Rack and power SKU.0.L.101.L12 Colocation Tier 3 - DC Geleen 2 - Geleen (NL) 1U Rack and power SKU.0.L.101.L03 Collocation Tier 3 - DC2011 - Hasselt (BE) – powerless 1U Rack (no power) SKU.0.L.101.L04 Collocation Tier 3 - DC1992 - Hasselt (BE) – powerless 1U Rack (no power) SKU.0.L.101.L13 Colocation Tier 3 - DC Geleen 1 - Geleen (NL) – powerless 1U Rack (no power) SKU.0.L.101.L14 Colocation Tier 3 - DC Geleen 2 - Geleen (NL) - powerless 1U Rack (no power) SKU.0.L.101.L05 Datacentre cage Cage – DC1992 – Hasselt (BE) Cage SKU.0.L.101.L15 Cage – DC Geleen 1 - Geleen (NL) Cage SKU.0.L.101.L42 Office space for DR DR office space – Hasselt (BE) Office SKU.0.L.101.L41 Datacentre Shared Blade slot Shared Full-Height blade slot - slot with underlaying redundant ethernet connection Blade slot with connectivity and power SKU.0.L.101.L06 Share Half-Height blade slot Tier 3 - DC2011 - Hasselt (BE) HH Blade slot and power SKU.0.L.101.L07 Shared Half-Height blade slot Tier 3 - DC1992 - Hasselt (BE) HH Blade slot and power SKU.0.L.101.L16 Shared Half-Height blade slot Tier 3 - DC Geleen 1 - Geleen (NL) HH Blade slot and power SKU.0.L.101.L17 Shared Half-Height blade slot Tier 3 - DC Geleen 1 - Geleen (NL) HH Blade slot and power SKU.0.L.101.L21 POP rack space Collocation POP Brussels 1U Rack and power SKU.0.L.101.L22 Collocation connectivity POP Brussels Patching in POP SKU.0.L.101.L31 Collocation POP Amsterdam 1U Rack and power SKU.0.L.101.L32 Collocation connectivity POP Amsterdam Patching in POP Measurement The quantity of each billing element is determined on a monthly basis: Calculation / Billing The price(s) is/are determined in the pricing schedule where this Service is residing. The Service is billed per billing element:

Section.name: Optional Service Billing
Section.Content: The service is billed on a monthly basis. ID Component Billing element BILLING UNIT SKU.0.L.101.L55 SAN connectivity SAN Fibre Channel Port (for fysical non-blade serers) (X000016) Per port SKU.0.L.101.L57 SAN Fibre Channel Gateway (for blade servers) (X000069) Per port SKU.0.L.101.L54 SAN storage for dedicated virtualization environments SAN Storage for dedicated virtualization environments Tier-Premium without replication (single DC) (X000259) Per GB SKU.0.L.101.L56 SAN Storage for dedicated virtualization environments Tier-Standard without replication (single DC) (X000260) Per GB SKU.0.L.101.L61 SAN storage (block storage) SAN Storage Tier-Premium without replication (single DC) (X000269) Per GB SKU.0.L.108.L62 SAN Storage Tier-General purpose without replication (single DC) (X000270) Per GB SKU.0.L.108.L58 SAN Storage Tier-High Bandwidth Storage without replication (single DC) (X000279) Per GB SKU.0.L.108.L59 SAN Storage Tier-Replication (X000015) Per replicated GB Measurement The quantity of each billing element is determined on a monthly basis: Calculation / Billing The price(s) is/are determined in the pricing schedule where this Service is residing. The Service is billed per billing element:

Section.name: Change Request Billing
Section.Content: Changes are billed on a monthly basis. Changes as result of Pre-approved and Normal Service requests are billed according to the defined prices in the pricing document of the related contact. Non-standard change requests are billed on T&M basis.

Section.name: Document control
Section.Content: Document version Date Author Notes DV1.0 13/3/2024 Lodewijk van der Sloot Johan Pauwels First version aligned with structural Service Description requirements. DV1.1 25/10/2024 Johan Pauwels Comments removed, document cleanup for PDF.
